_README: Verified, screen-safe facts. NEVER put a number on screen that isn't here, phrased as written. Verified June 2026. Each fact has a `safe_phrasing` (use verbatim or close) and a `do_not_say` guardrail.

ftc_fine.claim: BetterHelp shared mental-health data and was penalised
ftc_fine.safe_phrasing: In 2023 the US FTC ordered BetterHelp to pay $7.8 million and banned it from sharing users' sensitive mental-health data with Facebook and others for advertising — after promising it wouldn't.
ftc_fine.do_not_say: Don't claim it was a criminal conviction or an admission of guilt — it was a settlement; BetterHelp denied wrongdoing.

pay_gap.claim: Therapists keep well under half of what clients pay on big platforms
pay_gap.safe_phrasing: On the big platforms a client can pay $70–100 a week, while the therapist doing the work might see around a third of it.
pay_gap.do_not_say: Do NOT say 'they take 50%' or quote a fixed commission percentage — it's a flat per-engagement pay model, so frame it as a GAP, not a cut.

revenue.claim: BetterHelp generates around a billion dollars a year
revenue.safe_phrasing: BetterHelp generates around a billion dollars a year in revenue — for providing a platform.
revenue.do_not_say: Say REVENUE, not profit. Parent company Teladoc posted losses, so never say 'makes a billion in profit'.

client_cost.claim: Clients find the big platforms expensive
client_cost.safe_phrasing: Subscriptions on the big platforms commonly run around $280–400 a month.
client_cost.do_not_say: Use softly, as colour ('people online say it's pricey'), not as a hard headline stat.

faresay_model.claim: Faresay's own pricing — the fair alternative
faresay_model.safe_phrasing: Faresay is free to start, with an optional monthly plan that costs less than a single session. There is no commission on sessions — the only per-session cost is standard card processing, which Faresay keeps none of. Therapists keep their own clients, set their own prices, control their own data, and can run it from a phone.
faresay_model.do_not_say: Do NOT say '15%' or any commission percentage — that was the PRE-PIVOT model and is wrong now. Faresay takes no commission.

uk_therapy_cost.claim: Private therapy in the UK is expensive for many people
uk_therapy_cost.safe_phrasing: In the UK, a private therapy session usually costs somewhere between £50 and £90 for around 50 minutes.
uk_therapy_cost.do_not_say: It varies a lot by region, experience and type of therapy (psychologists cost more) — present it as a typical range, never a single fixed price. Never imply Faresay sets client prices; therapists set their own.

uk_waiting.claim: NHS mental-health waits in England are long
uk_waiting.safe_phrasing: In England, the longest waits for mental-health care now stretch close to two years — more than twice as long as the longest waits for physical-health treatment.
uk_waiting.do_not_say: This is the LONGEST waits, not the typical wait — never say 'everyone waits two years'. Never position Faresay as an NHS replacement or a crisis/emergency service.

uk_prevalence.claim: Mental-health need in England is widespread
uk_prevalence.safe_phrasing: In England, around 1 in 4 people experience a mental-health problem each year, and roughly 1 in 6 have a common problem like anxiety or depression in any given week.
uk_prevalence.do_not_say: Context only — never imply Faresay treats, cures or improves any of this. No clinical-outcome claims.

uk_special_category.claim: UK law gives mental-health data the highest protection
uk_special_category.safe_phrasing: In the UK, information about your mental health is 'special category' data under UK GDPR — the law gives it the highest level of protection.
uk_special_category.do_not_say: State the law, not a Faresay accreditation — don't claim a specific certification or ICO endorsement. Faresay is the processor; the therapist is the data controller.
